questions.question: What problem do AGENTS.md and SKILL.md solve for a workflow used across Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex?
options: They make the agents share one API key
Each agent had its own instruction format (slash-commands, rules, .codex.md) — these standards make one portable artifact replace three maintained copies
They compile prompts to bytecode
They encrypt the workflow
questions.explanation: The engineer's release-notes workflow had to be copied into each agent's proprietary format. AGENTS.md (read on session start) and SKILL.md (loaded by name on demand) travel unchanged across compatible agents.

questions.question: How do the three layers divide responsibilities?
options: AGENTS.md is for tests, SKILL.md for docs, MCP for deployment
AGENTS.md: project context and conventions; SKILL.md: reusable task know-how; MCP: the tools the skill invokes
All three are interchangeable formats for the same data
AGENTS.md and SKILL.md are deprecated in favor of MCP
questions.explanation: "What tools exist" is MCP; "how to do a task" is the skill; "how this project works" is AGENTS.md. The 2026 stack layers all three — one repo-level context file, portable skills, and a standard tool protocol.

questions.question: What does a SKILL.md consist of?
options: Pure YAML configuration
YAML frontmatter (name, description) plus a markdown body that becomes the model's prompt when the skill loads, optionally with bundled resources
A Python entry point and requirements.txt
A JSON Schema only
questions.explanation: Anthropic's Agent Skills (open standard, December 2025): frontmatter declares identity for discovery; the body is the instructions. Resources alongside the file enable progressive disclosure.

questions.question: What is progressive disclosure in skills?
options: Showing the user one line of output at a time
The skill references sub-resources (style guides, templates) that the agent fetches only when needed, keeping the initial context load small
Gradually increasing the model's temperature
Revealing the skill's source code after purchase
questions.explanation: A skill folder can hold style-guide.md and template.md next to SKILL.md; the agent loads the main body up front and pulls supporting files on demand — full depth without paying full context cost every time.

questions.question: What is AGENTS.md and how widely is it adopted?
options: An Anthropic-only configuration file
A repo-root file with project conventions and commands that agents read on session start — adopted by 60,000+ repos and supported by every major coding agent
A registry of public agents
A replacement for README.md
questions.explanation: Launched late 2025; by April 2026 Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Copilot Workspace, opencode, Windsurf, and Zed all read it — one place for "strict TypeScript, tests run with pnpm test" instead of per-agent config.
